content: The article is a 2019 review on early-onset Alzheimer’s disease (EOAD), emphasizing that EOAD is often driven by autosomal dominant mutations (APP, PSEN1, PSEN2) and may have distinct clinical and pathological features from late-onset AD. It highlights that EOAD patients are being prioritized in trials of disease-modifying therapies, especially anti-amyloid approaches (e.g., monoclonal antibodies, β- and γ-secretase modulators) and prevention trials in presymptomatic mutation carriers. The review also notes interest in tau-targeted therapies and other pathway-based interventions (e.g., targeting synaptic dysfunction and neuroinflammation), though these are mostly in early-phase or preclinical stages. Overall, the paper frames EOAD as a key population for testing mechanism-based treatments and prevention strategies due to its clearer genetic etiology and predictable disease course.
